Document:
# Super Mutants
[textlink="View Full Wiki Page" link="https://ss14.misfitsystems.net/wiki/index.php/Super%20Mutants"/]
◈ Super Mutants ◈ FEV Mutants ◈ Wendover Region ◈
[bold]Super Mutants[/bold]
◈ FACTION DOSSIER ◈
[bold]Status[/bold] Active - Scattered [bold]Location[/bold] Bonneville Salt Flats, Utah Wasteland Ruins [bold]Commander[/bold] N/A - Individual [bold]Ideology[/bold] Strength Above All [bold]Motto[/bold] [italic]Humans weak. Super Mutants strong.[/italic] [bold]Affiliation[/bold] Independent ⬜ RESTRICTED ACCESS ⬜
[bold]Super Mutants[/bold] are humans who were exposed to the [bold]Forced Evolutionary Virus (FEV)[/bold] - a pre-war biological agent designed to create the perfect soldier. The virus transformed them into massive, green-skinned creatures of immense physical power and near-total radiation immunity. The cost was severe: most lost higher cognitive function, becoming violent and territorial. Some, however, retained their intelligence - and with it, their capacity for choice.
## Physical Characteristics
[bold]1.5× human scale[/bold] - visually and physically larger than any human
Extreme physical strength and resilience
Near-total radiation immunity
Greatly extended lifespan (centuries, with no confirmed upper limit)
Skin coloration ranges from olive green to deep yellow-green
## Roles
[bold]Role[/bold] [bold]Description[/bold] [bold]Notes[/bold] Super Mutant An FEV-mutated human navigating the Wasteland. Your size is your advantage and your curse - feared by most, trusted by few. How you use your strength defines what you are. Whitelist required [bold]Super Mutant Gladiator[/bold] A Super Mutant who has embraced combat as purpose. Arena-tested, wasteland-hardened, and looking for the next challenge worthy of their attention. Whitelist required
Both roles require [bold]whitelist approval[/bold] and lock the player to the Super Mutant species with restricted customization options.
## Relations
Super Mutants occupy an uneasy position in the Wasteland. Most factions regard them with fear, hostility, or both. Individual Super Mutants may earn tolerance - or even grudging respect - through their actions, but the default assumption from every settlement and patrol is that a Super Mutant is a threat until proven otherwise.
[bold]Faction[/bold] [bold]Standing[/bold] [bold]Notes[/bold] [textlink="Brotherhood of Steel" link="https://ss14.misfitsystems.net/wiki/index.php/Brotherhood_of_Steel"/] [bold]Kill on Sight[/bold] The Brotherhood classifies Super Mutants as Biological Abominations. No exceptions. [textlink="NCR" link="https://ss14.misfitsystems.net/wiki/index.php/New_California_Republic"/] Hostile NCR policy treats Super Mutants as hostile wildlife. Individual soldiers may disagree privately. [textlink="Caesar's Legion" link="https://ss14.misfitsystems.net/wiki/index.php/Caesar%27s_Legion"/] Hostile The Legion does not tolerate what it cannot control. Super Mutants are too dangerous to enslave and too proud to submit. [textlink="Townsfolk" link="https://ss14.misfitsystems.net/wiki/index.php/Townsfolk"/] Fearful A Super Mutant in town clears the street. Earning trust takes time and restraint. [textlink="Wastelanders" link="https://ss14.misfitsystems.net/wiki/index.php/Wastelanders"/] Variable Some wastelanders will trade. Some will run. Some will shoot first. Other Super Mutants Neutral Shared blood does not guarantee shared purpose.
